title: The cubic case of the main conjecture in Vinogradov's mean value theorem

thm:1.1.summary: The cubic case of Vinogradov's main conjecture: for each $\varepsilon>0$, $J_{s,3}(X)\ll X^\varepsilon(X^s+X^{2s-6})$. The conjectured sharp bound for the degree-$3$ Vinogradov system, proved here by efficient congruencing — predating, and re-proved by different means in, the Bourgain–Demeter–Guth decoupling treatment of degrees $\ge4$.

thm:1.2.summary: Consequence for higher degree: for $k\ge3$ and $s\ge k(k-1)$, $J_{s,k}(X)\ll X^{2s-\frac12k(k+1)+\varepsilon}$. The main conjecture (in its 'large $s$' range) for all $k$, deduced by combining the cubic result with the efficient-congruencing toolkit.

lem:2.1.summary: Mean-value bound on the conditioned generating functions: for $u+v=6$ and $c,d\le\theta^{-1}$, $\oint|{\mathfrak f}_c(\boldsymbol\alpha;\xi)^{2u}{\mathfrak f}_d(\boldsymbol\alpha;\zeta)^{2v}|\,d\boldsymbol\alpha\ll(J(X/M^c))^{u/6}(J(X/M^d))^{v/6}$. Hölder + translation-dilation invariance reduces moments of the pieces to $J$ at coarser scales.

lem:3.1.summary: Congruence-solution count via the Jacobian: for polynomials $f_1,\dots,f_d$ with Jacobian $J(\mathbf f;\mathbf x)$, the number $\mathcal{N}(\mathbf f;\varpi^l)$ of solutions of $f_j\equiv0\pmod{\varpi^l}$ is controlled by Hensel-type lifting from the non-degenerate locus. The arithmetic engine of 'congruencing'.

lem:3.2.summary: Bounds on the basic congruence counts of the iteration: $B_{a,b}^{1,3}(p)\le6$ and $B_{a,b}^{2,h/b}(p)\le6p^{h-2b+a}$ for $2b-a\le h\le2b-a+\Delta(b-a)$. The combinatorial input from lem:3.1 in the precise form the recursion needs.

lem:4.1.summary: Efficient differencing step: for $1\le a<b$, $H\ge15$, $b+H\le(2\theta)^{-1}$, there is $0\le h<H$ with $I_{a,b}^2(X)\ll(M^h)^{8/3}K_{a,b+h}^2(X)+M^{-H}(X/M^b)^4(X/M^a)^{\lambda-4}$. The 'efficient congruencing' move that trades an $I$-quantity for a $K$-quantity one congruence level deeper, with negligible error.

lem:4.2.summary: Choice of auxiliary prime and shift: there is a prime $p\in(M,2M]$ and $0\le h\le4B$ with $J(X)\ll M^{8B+8h/3}K_{0,B+h}^2(X)$. Launches the iteration from the full mean value $J$ into the conditioned quantities.

lem:5.1.summary: The basic recursive inequalities: for $0\le a<b\le\theta^{-1}$ with $b\ge(1+\tfrac23\Delta)a$, $K_{a,b}^1(X)\ll M^{3b-a}(I_{b,3b}^2)^{1/4}(J(X/M^b))^{3/4}$ and $K_{a,b}^2(X)\ll M^{b'-2b+a}(M^{b'-a})^{4/3}(I_{b,b'}^2)^{1/3}(K_{a,b}^1)^{2/3}$. One cycle of the congruencing iteration.

lem:5.2.summary: Normalized form of the recursion: writing $[\![Q]\!]$ for the scale-normalized quantity, $[\![K_{a,b}^2(X)]\!]\ll((X/M^b)^{\Lambda+\delta})^{1/2}[\![I_{b,3b}^2]\!]^{1/6}[\![I_{b,b'}^2]\!]^{1/3}\cdots$. Recasts lem:5.1 in the form on which the descent operates.

lem:5.3.summary: $R$-fold iterate: for $0<a<b\le(16\cdot3^{2R}R\theta)^{-1}$ with $a\le b/(1+\tfrac23\Delta)$, there is a shift vector $\mathbf h(\mathbf m)\in\{0,1\}^R$ (with $0\le h_n\le15\cdot3^Rb$) for which the normalized $K_{a,b}^2$ is bounded by a product of deeper-level normalized quantities. Composes $R$ congruencing cycles.

lem:6.1.summary: Elementary Hölder-type inequality: if $\Omega=\sum_i\beta_i\gamma_i$ then $|z_0^{\beta_0}\cdots z_l^{\beta_l}|\le\sum_{i=0}^l|z_i|^{\Omega/\gamma_i}$. The convexity device that linearizes the products of mean values in the iterate.

lem:6.2.summary: The key iterative inequality: combining the $R$-fold iterate with the Hölder linearization gives, under the bookkeeping hypotheses ($0\le c\le(2\delta)^{-1}\theta$, $\gamma\ge-4b$, $\psi\ge0$), a self-referential bound on $[\![K_{a,b}^2(X)]\!]$ in terms of the same quantity at deeper levels with strictly improved exponents.

lem:7.1.summary: Closing the descent: provided $\Lambda\ge0$, $[\![K_{a,b}^2(X)]\!]\ll X^{\Lambda+\delta}$ for all $0\le a<b\le(2\theta)^{-1}$. Iterating lem:6.2 to infinity (the 'efficient congruencing' descent) forces this bound — the heart of the method.

thm:7.2.summary: For any $\Delta<\tfrac1{12}$ and $\varepsilon>0$, $J(X)=J_{6,3}(X)\ll X^{6+\Delta+\varepsilon}$. Feeding lem:4.2 the descent output lem:7.1 gives the critical mean value up to an arbitrarily small exponent gap.

cor:7.3.summary: $J(X)=J_{6,3}(X)\ll X^{6+\varepsilon}$ — let $\Delta\to0$ in thm:7.2. The critical-case main conjecture for the cubic Vinogradov system.

thm:8.1.summary: Asymptotic formula: for $s\ge7$, $J_s(X)\sim\mathfrak{S}_s\mathfrak{J}_sX^{2s-6}$ with the expected singular series/integral constants. Hardy–Littlewood asymptotics for the cubic Vinogradov system, now valid in the optimal range, via cor:7.3.

thm:8.2.summary: Application to cubic systems: for $s\ge13$ and nonzero integer coefficients with non-singular local solubility everywhere, the counting function $N(B)$ for the simultaneous cubic equations satisfies $N(B)\sim\sigma_\infty\prod_p\sigma_p\,B^{s-6}$, i.e. the Hasse principle and asymptotic hold. A Birch-type consequence.

edges.note: The general-$s$ cubic bound follows from the critical case $J_{6,3}(X)\ll X^{6+\varepsilon}$ by standard interpolation/Hölder over the number of variables.
The higher-degree large-$s$ bounds are obtained by feeding the cubic result into the efficient-congruencing machinery for general $k$.
Combine the launching inequality (lem:4.2) with the descent conclusion $[\![K^2]\!]\ll X^{\Lambda+\delta}$.
lem:4.2 reduces $J(X)$ to a conditioned quantity $K_{0,B+h}^2(X)$ at the start of the iteration.
The bound is forced by iterating the key self-referential inequality to infinity (the 'efficient congruencing' descent).
The self-referential bound is the $R$-fold composed recursion, unrolled.
The Hölder-type inequality linearizes the products of mean values appearing in the iterate.
Composes the normalized one-cycle recursion $R$ times with a $\{0,1\}^R$ shift vector.
Normalizes the basic recursive inequalities to the scale-invariant form.
Each cycle uses the efficient differencing step to descend one congruence level.
The mean-value bound on the conditioned pieces feeds the $I$/$J$ factors in the recursion.
The differencing step is controlled by the basic congruence-solution counts.
Launching the iteration uses one application of the differencing step at $a=0$.
The congruence-count bounds come from the Jacobian/Hensel solution-counting lemma.
The asymptotic formula follows from the sharp mean-value bound via the circle method.
The Birch-type counting asymptotic for cubic systems is deduced from the Vinogradov asymptotic.

main_results.description: The cubic case of Vinogradov's main conjecture and its higher-degree corollary.

congruencing_arithmetic.description: The arithmetic inputs: Jacobian/Hensel congruence-solution counting and the resulting basic bounds.

efficient_differencing_and_recursion.description: The efficient differencing step, the launch of the iteration, and the one-cycle and $R$-fold recursive inequalities (normalized and raw), plus the mean-value bound on conditioned pieces.

the_descent.description: The Hölder linearization, the key self-referential inequality, the descent to $[\![K^2]\!]\ll X^{\Lambda+\delta}$, and the resulting bound on the critical mean value.

applications.description: Hardy–Littlewood asymptotics for the cubic Vinogradov system and a Birch-type asymptotic for systems of cubic equations.

Wooley:Quasidiagonal.title: Vinogradov's mean value theorem via efficient congruencing

Wooley:EfficientCongruencingII.title: Vinogradov's mean value theorem via efficient congruencing, II
